name: Notify stakeholders on changes with Microsoft Teams

parameters.text: =✅ Notify stakeholders on changes with Microsoft Teams — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf09454-event`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Normalize Event Payload

### Normalize Event Payload

Custom JavaScript: Normalize incoming event records to a stable schema

Next: Route by Work Type

### Route by Work Type

Routes each item to one of 3 lanes: standard, priority + fallback.

Ports: ①→ Process 'standard' → Google Sheets · ②→ Fast-Track 'priority' + Alert · ③→ Hold Unknown Types (DLQ)

### Process 'standard' → Google Sheets

Upserts a row into 'Event Tracker', matched on `eventId` — re-running with the same key updates the row instead of duplicating it.

Ports: ①→ Merge Routed Work · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Fast-Track 'priority' + Alert

Posts a chat message into a Microsoft Teams channel — same role as a Slack notify, for teams standardized on Teams.

Ports: ①→ Merge Routed Work · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

### Hold Unknown Types (DLQ)

Appends a new row to 'Event Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Merge Routed Work · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Merge Routed Work

Rejoins 3 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Save Execution Metadata

### Save Execution Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Stamp Processing Time

### Stamp Processing Time

Stamps `processedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Send Summary to Slack

### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#alerts`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#alerts`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

# Notify stakeholders on changes with Microsoft Teams

**Pattern:** Event Notification · **Level:** Core · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Notify stakeholders on changes with Microsoft Teams. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual digests is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Normalize Event Payload → Route by Work Type → Process 'standard' → Google Sheets → Merge Routed Work → Save Execution Metadata → Stamp Processing Time → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to event notification without manual effort
- Anyone automating digests in software development
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Standardizes the process every time
- Responds in seconds
- Has retries and error handling built in

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google OAuth2 / service account; Microsoft OAuth2; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Unrecognized work types are parked in a dead-letter lane instead of erroring the whole run or silently dropping the item — new event kinds will show up eventually, and this is where you'd notice one without losing it. One kind is deliberately routed through a sub-workflow to show how heavier per-type logic can be extracted without touching this router at all.

## Practice exercises

Once it's wired up with real credentials, try:

1. Send a work-type value that matches no switch rule and confirm it lands in the dead-letter branch.
2. Add a new work type and its own handling branch.
3. Replace the direct-write branch with a sub-workflow call, matching the pattern already used for the first kind.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your digests process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
